==Further reading==
* Abernethy, Thomas P. ''The South in the New Nation, 1789–1819'' (LSU Press, 1961) [https://archive.org/details/southinnewnation00thom online]
* Alden, John R. ''The South in the Revolution, 1763–1789'' (LSU Press, 1957) [https://archive.org/details/southinrevolutio0000alde online]
* Ayers; Edward L. ''The Promise of the New South: Life after Reconstruction'' (Oxford University Press, 1993). [https://archive.org/details/promiseofnewso00ayer online]
* Bartley, Numan V. ''The New South, 1945–1980'' (LSU Press, 1996) [https://archive.org/details/newsouth194519800000bart online]
* Best, John Hardin. "Education in the Forming of the American South". ''History of Education Quarterly'' (1996) 36#1 pp.&nbsp;39–51 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/369300 at JSTOR]
* Clark, Thomas D. ''Pills, Petticoats, and Plows: The Southern Country Store'' (1944).
* [[William J. Cooper Jr.|Cooper, William J.]], Thomas E. Terrill & Christopher Childers. ''The American South'' (2 vols. 5th ed. 2016), 1160 pp [https://archive.org/details/isbn_9780394589480 online 1991 edition]
* [[William J. Cooper Jr.|Cooper, William J.]] ''Liberty and Slavery : Southern Politics to 1860'' (U. of South Carolina Press, 2000).
* Craven, Avery O. ''The Growth of Southern Nationalism, 1848–1861'' (LSU Press, 1953) [https://archive.org/details/growthofsouthern0000crav online]
* Craven, Wesley Frank. ''The Southern Colonies in the Seventeenth Century, 1607–1689. '' (LSU Press, 1949) [https://archive.org/details/southerncolonies00wesl online]
* Current, Richard, (ed.) ''Encyclopedia of the Confederacy'' (4 vols, 1995); 1,474 entries by 330 scholars. 
* {{cite book |last=Davis |first=William C. |title=Look Away! A History of the Confederate States of America |location=New York |publisher=The Free Press |year=2003 |isbn=0-684-86585-8 |url=https://archive.org/details/lookawayhistoryo00will}}
* Ferris, William & Charles Reagan Wilson, (eds.) ''Encyclopedia of Southern Culture'' (1990) 1630pp; comprehensive coverage. 
** ''The New Encyclopedia of Southern Culture'' (2013) 25 volumes of about 400 pages each provides intense coverage. [https://www.amazon.com/New-Encyclopedia-Southern-Culture-Folk/dp/0807871745/ sample volume on "Folk Art".]
* Foner, Eric. ''Reconstruction: America's Unfinished Revolution, 1863–1877'' (2014) [https://archive.org/details/isbn_9780060158514 online]
* Fry, Joseph. ''Dixie Looks Abroad: The South and U.S. Foreign Relations, 1789—1973'' (2002)
* Giggie, John & Andrew J. Huebner, (eds.) ''Dixie's Great War: World War I and the American South. War, Memory, and Culture'' (U of Alabama Press, 2020) [https://www.h-net.org/reviews/showpdf.php?id=58651 online book review]
* Gray, Lewis C. ''History of Agriculture in the Southern United States to 1860'' ( 2 vols. 1933) [https://archive.org/details/historyofagricul0001gray/page/n6/mode/1up vol 1 online]; also see [https://archive.org/details/historyofagricul0000gray/page/n6/mode/1up vol 2 online]
* Hill, Samuel S. et al. (eds.) ''Encyclopedia of Religion in the South'' (2005)
* Hubbell, Jay B. ''The South in American Literature, 1607–1900'' (Duke UP, 1973) [https://archive.org/details/southinamericanl0000unse online]
* Key, V.O. ''Southern Politics in State and Nation'' (1951) classic political analysis, state by state. [https://archive.org/search.php?query=title%3A%28Statistical%20atlas%20of%20southern%20counties%29 online free to borrow]
* Kirby, Jack Temple. ''Rural Worlds Lost: The American South, 1920–1960'' (LSU Press, 1986) major scholarly survey with detailed bibliography; [https://archive.org/details/ruralworldslosta0000kirb online free to borrow].
* Kulikoff, Allan. ''Tobacco and slaves: The development of southern cultures in the Chesapeake, 1680–1800'' (UNC Press Books, 2012) [https://books.google.com/books?id=taPqCQAAQBAJ&dq=Kulikoff&pg=PP1 online].
* Lamis, Alexander P. (ed.) ''Southern Politics in the 1990s'' (LSU Press, 1999).
*[[Rayford Logan|Logan, Rayford]], ''The Betrayal of the Negro from Rutherford B. Hayes to Woodrow Wilson'', (1997). (This is an expanded edition of Logan, ''The Negro in American Life and Thought, The Nadir, 1877–1901'' (1954)) [https://archive.org/details/betrayalofnegrof0000loga online]
* Mark, Rebecca, & Rob Vaughan. ''The South: The Greenwood Encyclopedia of American Regional Cultures'' (2004). Post-1945 society.
* Marrs, Aaron W. ''Railroads in the Old South: Pursuing Progress in a Slave Society'' (2009)
* Moreland; Laurence W. et al. ''Blacks in Southern Politics'' Praeger Publishers, 1987
* Paterson, Thomas G. (ed.) ''Major Problems in the History of the American South'' (1999). Readings from primary and secondary sources.
* Raffel, Jeffrey. ''Historical dictionary of school segregation and desegregation: The American experience'' (Bloomsbury, 1998) [https://books.google.com/books?id=2a7OEAAAQBAJ&dq=HISTORICAL+DICTIONARY+OF+SCHOOL+SEGREGATION+AND+DESEGREGATION&pg=PR7 online]
* Richter, William L. ''The A to Z of the Old South'' (2009), a short scholarly encyclopedia
* Roller, David C. & Robert W. Twyman, (eds.) ''Encyclopedia of Southern History'' (1979) 1420 pp.&nbsp;Comprehensive brief coverage of 3000 topics by 1000+ scholars. [https://archive.org/details/encyclopediaofso0000unse_d5d4 online]
* Shafer, Byron E. & Richard Johnston, (eds.) ''The End of Southern Exceptionalism: Class, Race, and Partisan Change in the Postwar South'' (2009) [https://www.amazon.com/End-Southern-Exceptionalism-Partisan-Postwar/dp/0674032497/ excerpt and text search]
* Sydnor, Charles W. ''The Development of Southern Sectionalism, 1819–1848''. (LSU Press, 1964). Broad-ranging history of the region [https://archive.org/details/developmentofsou0000sydn_t7b9 online]
* Tindall, George B. ''The Emergence of the New South, 1913–1945'' (LSU Press, 1967) [https://archive.org/details/emergenceofnewso00tind online]
* Tucker, Spencer, (ed.) ''American Civil War: A State-by-State Encyclopedia'' (2 vols, 2015) 1019pp [https://www.amazon.com/American-Civil-volumes-State---State/dp/1598845284/ excerpt]
* Volo, James M. ''Encyclopedia of the Antebellum South'' (2000)
* {{cite book |last=Woodward |first=C. Vann |title=The Origins of the New South |publisher=Louisiana State University Press |year=1951}} A classic history [https://archive.org/details/originsofnewsout00wood online]

===Historiography===
* {{cite book |editor1-first=John B |editor1-last=Boles |editor2-first=Bethany L. |editor2-last=Johnson |title='Origins of the New South' Fifty Years Later |year=2003}}. Historiography re C. Vann Woodward.
* Boles, John B., (ed.) ''A Companion to the American South'' (2008). Emphasis on historiography.
* Boles, John B. & Evelyn Thomas Nolen, (eds.) ''Interpreting Southern History: Historiographical Essays in Honor of Sanford W. Higginbotham'' (1987). A major collection of essays by scholars on leading themes.
* Boles, John, et al. (eds.) ''Shapers of Southern History: Autobiographical Reflections'' (2004)
* Feldman, Glenn, (ed.) ''Reading southern history: essays on interpreters and interpretations'' (U. of Alabama Press, 2001).
* Goldfield, David. ''Still Fighting the Civil War: The American South and Southern History'' (2013)
* Link, Arthur S., et al. ''Writing Southern history; essays in historiography in honor of Fletcher M. Green'' (1965). Essays by experts on the historiography of the main topics.
* Rabinowitz, Howard N., & James Michael Russell. "What Urban History Can Teach Us About the South and the South Can Teach Us About Urban History." ''Georgia Historical Quarterly'' (1989) 73#1 pp.&nbsp;54–66 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/40581930 at JSTOR]
* Stephenson, Wendell Holmes (ed.) ''Southern History in the Making: Pioneer Historians of the South'' (1964).

===Primary sources===
* Clark, Thomas D. ''Travels in the New South, 1865–1955: A Bibliography'' (2 vols. 1962). An annotated bibliography of about 1000 books published by American and European travelers in the South. Discusses the background of the author, the content, the authors viewpoint or bias, and the quality of the information. Some titles are on line at books.google.com
* Clark, Thomas D. ''Travels in the Old South'' (3 vols. 1956–59). An annotated bibliography of about 1300 books published by travelers in the South before 1865. Discusses the background of the author, the content, the authors viewpoint or bias, and the quality of the information. Some titles are on line at books.google.com
* Johnson, Charles S. ''Statistical Atlas of Southern Counties: Listing and analysis of socio-economic indices of 1104 southern counties'' (1941). [https://books.google.com/books?id=ATjQAAAAMAAJ excerpt]
* [[Ulrich Bonnell Phillips|Phillips, Ulrich B.]] ''Plantation and Frontier Documents, 1649–1863; Illustrative of Industrial History in the Colonial and Antebellum South: Collected from MSS. and Other Rare Sources.'' (2 vols. 1909). vol 1 & 2 online edition 716pp

==External links==
* [http://docsouth.unc.edu/index.html Documenting the American South] – text, image, and audio collections.
* [https://www.jstor.org/action/showPublication?journalCode=JSouthernhistory ''Journal of Southern History'' articles at JSTOR]
* [http://sha.uga.edu/ Southern Historical Association] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211118132715/http://sha.uga.edu/ |date=November 18, 2021 }}, The major scholarly society.
